title: Value-Added Services | Fragomen, Del Rey, Bernsen & Loewy LLP
content:
## Value-Added Services

### Enrich your immigration service experience.
At Fragomen, our clients are our partners. Together, we’re navigating a fluctuating and challenging immigration landscape. Together, we’re working to achieve our business goals.
Every successful partnership starts with trust and a commitment to communication and transparency. And every successful immigration program requires much more than transactional application support.
As part of our commitment to this partnership, Fragomen provides a range of value-added services at no cost to enrich your immigration service experience.

### Timely updates and alerts.
When significant changes in immigration regulations, requirements, policies or procedures are anticipated or implemented, we will send you Immigration Alerts and updates. You can sign up to receive alerts automatically, or access current or past alerts at any time on our website.
We also provide clients with a range of helpful knowledge resources for the countries where we serve you such as country snapshots, process flows, videos and document checklists.

### Case management technology.
To make the complex simpler, our proprietary, state-of-the-art case management technology— Fragomen Connect —will give you 24/7 access to your case matters and serve as a central resource for information transfer and document upload and storage. Whether you need to see a specific population for program management purposes or a specific case matter for an employee and their dependents, our system is a one-stop shop for all of your case and program management needs.

### Reporting and analytics.
Reporting is a critical tool for program managers. Fragomen offers a suite of standard reports free of charge, including extensive dashboard analytics of your immigration program and population.

### Business review meetings.
Meeting periodically to discuss big picture strategies and anticipated needs is an important part of keeping your immigration program on-course. These meetings are designed to:
- Assess the success of your program and service delivery
- Review changes to corporate goals, policies or practices that could affect your immigration program
- Analyze and determine potential areas for program enhancements

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
